repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest catalogue entries stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap and stat_dose_response_minimax address different estimands and risk criteria

published_prior_art.cite: XuGuo2026, WhitehouseChenAusternSyrgkanis2025, and WuWang2021 all give optimal-regime/value inference; none located gives this finite-union expected-length claim

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry studies honest interval length for an optimal-policy value

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004 supplies ordered-modulus expected-length lower bounds for linear Gaussian functionals, not this causal maximum
LuedtkeVanderLaan2016 already treats exceptional versus non-exceptional optimal-value inference; the proposed plateau calculation is an unverified specialization
CaiLow2004, XuGuo2026, WhitehouseChenAusternSyrgkanis2025, and WuWang2021 do not state the requested causal tie--smoothness ordered-modulus diagram

structure_flags.label: thm:uniform-validity-upper-length / project kernel
structure_flags.one_line: The strongest stated result remains a to-prove theorem rather than a conjecture; relabel the headline frontier assertion as a conjecture after repairing its mathematical defects.

novelty_flags.label: thm:uniform-validity-upper-length
novelty_flags.one_line: WuWang2021 is a closer omitted optimal-regime comparator: it establishes asymptotically accurate inference for the optimal value using a smoothed robust estimator, so the survey must distinguish the finite-union honest-length target from that result.

novelty_flags.label: thm:uniform-validity-upper-length and thm:two-regime-diagram
novelty_flags.one_line: The current statements give a known-estimand coverage/length claim but neither a stated strict improvement over a named bound nor a valid matched lower--upper frontier or matching limit-law calibration; the stated ordered-modulus ratio does not supply that comparison.

soundness_flags.label: lem:nonempty-strata / def:rademacher-members
soundness_flags.one_line: Admissibility leaves delta_j unbounded and imposes no compatibility with B, v_0, or L_j, yet the proposed member fixes m_1=2delta_j and must satisfy |m_1|<=B/2 and Var(Y(1)|X)>=v_0; thus the claimed witness cannot exist for every admissible zeta_j.

soundness_flags.label: thm:ordered-modulus-lower-bound
soundness_flags.one_line: The universal quantifier permits P_n=Q_n, making the displayed expected-length ratio undefined, and P_t is never constructed as a function of t in any definition.

soundness_flags.label: thm:ordered-modulus-lower-bound
soundness_flags.one_line: The consequence L^{asy}_{j,n}/omega^+_{j,n}>=c_gamma does not follow: the pairwise premise only controls H(P_n,Q_n)<=1/sqrt(8n), whereas omega^+ is defined at the larger radius 1/sqrt(n), with no modulus-scaling or class-convexity argument bridging the two radii.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article proves a pathwise-differentiability characterization and describes root-n confidence intervals for the optimal value, including non-pathwise-differentiable cases.
The preprint develops adaptive smoothing, valid inference regardless of regularity, and an attained asymptotic-variance lower bound within robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimators.

source_verification_receipts.version: author-hosted arXiv version corresponding to Annals of Statistics 32(5), 2004
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The work concerns adaptive confidence intervals for linear functionals, using an ordered modulus and expected-length lower bounds.

source_verification_receipts.version: author preprint corresponding to Annals of Statistics 35(2), 2007
source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 18 and 25, Lemma 5.1 and margin-condition construction
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper uses Hölder regression geometry and a Tsybakov-style margin condition, with sup-norm regression-rate consequences for plug-in classification.
The preprint constructs a softmax-smoothing estimator for optimal-policy values, with a constant number of nuisance fits and efficiency in the zero-nonresponse regime.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 112(520), 2017
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper studies rilpivirine versus efavirenz and individualized regimes based on baseline viral load and CD4 cell count.
The paper gives resampling-based asymptotically accurate inference for both an optimal-regime parameter and its optimal value function using a smoothed robust estimator.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the admissible parameter set with explicit feasibility inequalities linking delta_j, B, v_0, L_j, p-range, and the desired plateau construction; then state nonemptiness only for that restricted set.

Replace the lower-bound conclusion by a modulus defined at the testing radius H<=1/sqrt(8n), or prove a genuine radius-comparison lemma under explicitly stated closure/convexity conditions before comparing it with omega^+_{j,n}.

Add Wu and Wang (2021) and state a theorem-level distinction: either a strict finite-union honest-length improvement over its inference result or a matched causal lower/upper adaptation frontier with an explicit tie-experiment critical constant.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a mathematical redesign: repair stratum feasibility and the ordered-modulus lower-bound logic before reconsidering this angle.
